commit.message:
Pulse start per byte: add REQ_DATA state for dataConsume handshake

dataConsume needs a start pulse for each byte, not just once at the
beginning. After transmitting each streaming byte and dataReady goes
low, the FSM now enters REQ_DATA which pulses start before returning
to WAIT_DATA. This matches the reference design's req_data state.

commit.message:
Revision #1 (#1)

* Refactor GameTheory layout and add InfoModel Kuhn scaffold

* tmp

* Prove full InfoModel Kuhn via model-level step-independence bridge

* Prove InfoModel Kuhn behavioral-to-mixed directly

* Refactor profiles to depend only on local observed traces

* Prove observation-equality implies obsEq with explicit compatibility hypothesis

* Kuhn: isolate remaining posterior gap and clean theorem warnings

* Complete Kuhn mixed-to-behavioral proof

* Refactor Kuhn theorem structure

* Trim Kuhn core aliases

* Extract Kuhn local conditioning lemmas

* Move Kuhn step locality lemmas

* Extract Kuhn reach factorization support

* Clean Kuhn theorem docs and local history helpers

* Deduplicate Kuhn witness equality lemma

* Add prefix continuation execution kernels

* Add prefix-suffix local history composition lemmas

* Add prefix-conditioned Kuhn behavioral tail agreement

* Add continuation support length lemma

* Package prefix-conditioned Kuhn step agreement

* Add Kuhn prefix conditioning composition lemma

* Add conditioned one-step Kuhn realization theorem

* Attempt Kuhn continuation refactor

* Update PMF product notes and local sufficiency

* Propagate public-view Kuhn semantics

* Export Kuhn reachable-prefix bridge lemmas

* Add Kuhn sequencing distributive law

* Add explicit Kuhn continuation law

* Route Kuhn bridge through continuation law

* Generalize Kuhn local continuation law

* Derive Kuhn sequencing law from explicit continuation

* Use strongest Kuhn continuation theorem in bridge

* Delete dead Kuhn prefix helper subtree

* Factor Kuhn query disintegration core

* Update EFG compilation and Kuhn corollary

* Move Kuhn factorization into model lemmas

* Update EFG tests for compiled semantics

* Restore Sequential and MAID modules

* Reorganize Sequential and MAID under Languages

* Archive sequential proto proofs as reference text

* Separate language examples from tests

* Normalize language entrypoints and bridge shims

* Add native SOS and reductions for Sequential

* Add frontier SOS and reductions for MAID

* Shallow EFG wrapper namespaces and shims

* Add compiled Kuhn reduction for Sequential

* Add restricted profile layer for Kuhn covers

* Add restricted product factorization for Kuhn

* Add restricted Kuhn theorem core

* Add correlated realization theorem via parameterized chain tower property

Math/ParameterizedChain.lean: abstract tower property for parameterized
Markov chains — conditioning distributes over sequential Kleisli composition.

GameTheory/Theorems/CorrelatedRealization.lean: thin game-theoretic reduction
producing a mediator (correlated action recommendations) matching any mixed
pure-profile distribution. No perfect recall needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Expose restricted Kuhn theorem reductions

* Weaken Kuhn local finiteness assumptions

* Add bounded history cover API

* Prove evaluation invariance under history covers

* Add cover-based Kuhn wrappers

* Refactor game targets and finite-cover reductions

* Prefer canonical cover reductions and trim shims

* Add native simulation contracts for compilations

* Add PerStepPlayerRecall, obs correlated realization, and reach structure

- obs_correlated_realization: under PSAR, lift state-trace mediator to
  BehavioralProfileCorr (observation-level correlated realization)
- PerStepPlayerRecall: per-player version of PerStepActionRecall where
  each player's action component is determined by own observation transition
- evalBehavioral_eq_of_stepIndependence: generalize KuhnCore's
  step-independence theorem to arbitrary ν : PMF (PureProfile I)
- pureStep_component_eq_of_pspr: per-player action depends only on
  own observation under PSPR
- pureRun_length: traces with nonzero pureRun have length n + 1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add experimental enabled-control semantics sketch

* Prove per-player reach factoring via Function.update

pureRun_nonzero_iff_update: under PSAR, reach nonzero iff
each single-player coordinate update is nonzero. This is the
cleanest per-player factoring, enabling product preservation
proofs for the correlated→independent Kuhn direction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Prove reweightPMF preserves product structure

reweightPMF_pmfPi: reweighting a product PMF by product weights
gives the product of reweighted marginals. Uses Fubini (prod_sum)
and ENNReal.prod_inv_distrib.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Prove product-in product-out for mediator under PSAR

Complete the decentralization direction: when the input strategy
distribution ν is a product (pmfPi σ), the mediator's output action
distribution is also a product, under PerStepActionRecall.

Key results:
- pureRun_cross_mul_product: pointwise cross-multiplication identity
  for reach weights vs per-player update weights
- mediator_product_of_product: the mediator preserves product structure,
  proven via reweightPMF factoring (cross-mul → product weight →
  pmfPi_push_coordwise)

The proof handles both degenerate (zero/infinite mass → reweightPMF
fallback) and non-degenerate cases cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Prove obs-locality of per-player consistency under PSAR

Add `pureRun_update_obs_local`: under PerStepActionRecall, the
consistency condition `pureRun (update π₀ i πᵢ) ss ≠ 0` depends
on the trace ss only through `projectStates i ss`. This is the
key lemma for observation-locality of mediator factors.

Also fix decentralization bridge theorems:
- `stepDistCorr_eq_stepDist_of_product` uses `simp only` instead of `rw`
- `evalIndep_of_corrProduct` uses `change` to convert between
  `runDist` and `seqRun` (definitionally equal)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Prove generalized Kuhn M→B under PerStepPlayerRecall

Complete the mixed-to-behavioral direction of Kuhn's theorem under
PSPR: for any product distribution over pure profiles, there exists
an independent behavioral profile producing the same outcome.

Key additions:
- reweightPMF_update_obs_local: per-player factor obs-locality (PSAR)
- obsEq_of_projectStates_append: helper for obs-eq extraction
- pureRun_update_obs_local_pspr: support equiv with different π₀
- reweightPMF_update_obs_local_pspr: factor obs-locality (PSPR)
- mixedToMediator_eq_pmfPi_factor: concrete product factorization
- kuhn_mixed_to_behavioral_pspr: main theorem (zero sorrys)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add per-player recall decomposition and Kuhn hierarchy

- Define PlayerStepRecall I i (single-player step recall)
- Prove PerStepPlayerRecall ↔ ∀ i, PlayerStepRecall I i
- Add per-player obs-locality lemmas (PSAR + PlayerStepRecall I i):
  pureRun_update_obs_local_player, reweightPMF_update_obs_local_player
- Add pureStep_component_eq_of_playerRecall (per-player version)
- Document 5-level Kuhn theorem hierarchy (correlated → obs-correlated
  → product preservation → per-player obs-locality → full decentralization)
- Add kuhn_mixed_to_behavioral_decomposed (per-player formulation)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Characterize exact weakest condition for Kuhn M→B

Define ReachablePlayerStepRecall: PlayerStepRecall restricted to
step-reachable source states. This is the exact weakest condition
the Kuhn M→B proof needs, strictly weaker than both PSPR and
PerfectRecall but implied by both.

New definitions:
- StepReachable: state reachable from init via M.step transitions
- ReachablePlayerStepRecall: per-player action determinism at
  reachable obs-equivalent transitions

Key theorems:
- PlayerStepRecall.toReachable: PSPR → reachable version (trivial)
- PerfectRecall.toReachablePlayerStepRecall: ActionRecall gives
  equal action traces at obs-equiv reachable endpoints, hence
  equal last actions
- pureRun_nonzero_to_reachActionTrace: bridge from pureRun support
  to ReachActionTrace witnesses
- pureRun_nonzero_last_stepReachable: last state of supported
  pureRun trace is step-reachable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Formalize semantic vs syntactic conditions for Kuhn M→B

Define semantic conditions (dynamics-dependent) and prove their
relationship to the syntactic conditions (dynamics-independent):

- ObsLocalFeasibility D i: continuation feasibility is obs-local
- StepActionDeterminism D: action uniquely determined at transitions
- PSAR → StepActionDeterminism (reflexive obs-equivalence)
- PSAR + PlayerStepRecall I i → ObsLocalFeasibility D i
- PSAR + ReachablePlayerStepRecall I i → ObsLocalFeasibility D i
- PSPR → ∀ i, ObsLocalFeasibility D i

The weakest syntactic → semantic bridge uses ReachablePlayerStepRecall,

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add TracePlayerStepRecall: tighter condition for Kuhn M→B

TracePlayerStepRecall I i requires action agreement only at transitions
from states reached via traces with equal full observation histories
(projectStates i ss = projectStates i ss'), not merely obs-equivalent
endpoints (obsEq i s s'). This is strictly weaker than
ReachablePlayerStepRecall because equal full obs-traces implies endpoint
obs-equiv but not conversely.

The Kuhn proof's induction naturally maintains the full trace equality
invariant, so TracePlayerStepRecall suffices:

  PlayerStepRecall → ReachablePlayerStepRecall → TracePlayerStepRecall
  PerfectRecall → ReachablePlayerStepRecall → TracePlayerStepRecall
  PSAR + TracePlayerStepRecall I i → ObsLocalFeasibility D i

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Semantic vs syntactic conditions, product preservation, TracePlayerStepRecall

Define semantic conditions (dynamics-dependent) and prove their
relationship to syntactic conditions (dynamics-independent):

- ObsLocalFeasibility D i: continuation feasibility is obs-local
- StepActionDeterminism D: action uniquely determined at transitions
- PSAR + TracePlayerStepRecall I i → ObsLocalFeasibility D i

TracePlayerStepRecall is the tightest syntactic per-player condition:
requires action agreement only at transitions from states reached via
traces with equal full observation histories, not merely obs-equivalent
endpoints.

  PlayerStepRecall → ReachablePlayerStepRecall → TracePlayerStepRecall
  PerfectRecall → ReachablePlayerStepRecall → TracePlayerStepRecall

Product preservation (conditioning_preserves_product): under PSAR,
if the ex ante distribution is a product, conditioning on reaching
any trace gives a product at the strategy level. The reach weight
is cross-multiplicatively equivalent to a per-player product weight.

Fix #10: Add async locks to prevent concurrent pull file conflicts (#41)

* Fix #10: Add async locks to prevent concurrent pull file conflicts

Add per-model asyncio.Lock to ModelStore.pull() so concurrent pulls of
the same model are serialized. The second caller re-checks is_downloaded()
after acquiring the lock and takes the "already downloaded" fast path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address review: pre-lock fast path and setdefault for _pull_lock

- Restore double-checked locking: is_downloaded() check before lock
  (fast path) avoids unnecessary serialization for already-downloaded
  models, with re-check inside lock as TOCTOU guard
- Use dict.setdefault() in _pull_lock() for atomic check-and-insert

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address review: clean up pull locks on delete, document double-locking

- delete() now pops stale _pull_locks entry for the deleted model
- Comment at asyncio.to_thread call explains the two-layer locking
  (asyncio lock for async pull path, threading lock for sync load path)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert lock cleanup in delete() to avoid race with in-flight pulls

Popping the lock from _pull_locks during delete() can race with a
pull() suspended at ensure_downloaded(). A new pull() would create
a fresh uncontended lock and start a concurrent download. Lock
entries are cheap (one per ever-pulled model), so don't clean them up.

fix: Route popup management through server API to fix homepage display

Root cause: HomepagePopupLayer called DB.getHomepagePopups() which uses
client-side Supabase. Public homepage visitors have no Supabase config
in localStorage, so isConfigured()=false and MOCK_DATA([]) was returned
— popups never appeared even when correctly created in admin.

Changes:
- Add GET /api/popups — server-side fetch using serverSupabase env vars;
  public gets active-only, admin?=1 gets all popups
- Add POST /api/popups — server-side popup creation
- Add PUT/DELETE /api/popups/[id] — server-side update and delete
- HomepagePopupLayer: switch from DB.getHomepagePopups() to /api/popups
  with client supabase config as fallback; keeps client-side date/dismiss filtering
- HomepagePopupManager: use server API for all CRUD operations so admin
  actions reliably reach DB regardless of env var configuration

commit.message:
RP2350 Pico port sprint plans (6 sprints) (#62)

* docs(plans): add 5 sprint plans for RP2350 Pico port

Add comprehensive sprint plans for porting PolySynth to Raspberry Pi
Pico 2 W (RP2350) with Waveshare Pico-Audio HAT:

- Sprint Pico-1: Toolchain & Hello World (SDK, CMake, blink + serial)
- Sprint Pico-2: I2S Audio Hello World (PIO I2S, DMA, 440Hz sine)
- Sprint Pico-3: Core DSP Integration (Engine → I2S, all voices)
- Sprint Pico-4: Effects Architecture (deploy flags, soft clipper)
- Sprint Pico-5: Serial Control Interface (REPL, Python controller)

Each plan includes TDD test specifications, acceptance criteria,
implementation guidance for juniors, review checklists, and
troubleshooting guides.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01GXAXwgqKVKi93hU8UCybH5

* docs(plans): add Sprint Pico-2 CI/CD & Emulation Pipeline, renumber sprints

Insert a new Sprint Pico-2 dedicated to establishing a two-layer
automated testing pipeline:

- Layer 1: Native host DSP tests with embedded config flags
  (POLYSYNTH_USE_FLOAT, MAX_VOICES=4, SEA_PLATFORM_EMBEDDED)
- Layer 2a: ARM cross-compile check (RP2350 .uf2 production)
- Layer 2b: Wokwi RP2040 emulation as behavioral proxy with
  firmware self-test markers ([TEST:ALL_PASSED])

Key findings documented: neither Wokwi nor Renode officially
support RP2350 yet. RP2040 used as CI proxy — SDK APIs are
source-compatible between RP2040 and RP2350.

Sprint renumbering:
  Pico-1: Toolchain (unchanged)
  Pico-2: CI/CD & Emulation (NEW)
  Pico-3: I2S Audio (was Pico-2)
  Pico-4: Core DSP Integration (was Pico-3)
  Pico-5: Effects Architecture (was Pico-4)
  Pico-6: Serial Control (was Pico-5)

All subsequent sprints now include CI Mandate block requiring:
- just test + just test-embedded pass
- just pico-build ARM compile succeeds
- Wokwi emulation detects [TEST:ALL_PASSED]

https://claude.ai/code/session_01GXAXwgqKVKi93hU8UCybH5
